message:
Add computation migration system with lease handoffs, checkpoints, and state machine

Introduces the full normative computation migration subsystem for moving
live computations between mesh nodes with crash-safe fencing guarantees.

checkpoint.rs:
- Add MigrationCapabilityContext carrying capability token JTI, checkpoint
  binding, sequence number, and audit event reference across migrations
- Add ChunkedObjectManifest and ChunkedCheckpoint for chunked transfer of
  large checkpoint payloads exceeding the 256KB threshold
- Add ComputationCheckpoint as the canonical wire type for checkpoint
  transfer, binding zone, computation ID, holder, fencing token, state
  payload, and capability context
- Add CheckpointTransferEncoding enum (Inline vs Chunked) for wire format
  negotiation
- Add ComputationMigrationError with variants for expired leases, stale
  fencing tokens, zone/holder mismatches, and checkpoint integrity failures
- Implement CBOR canonical serialization via CanonicalSerializer trait

connector_state.rs:
- Add MigratableComputationState explicit state machine with Running,
  Suspended, Transferring, Completed, and Failed states
- Add MigratableComputation struct tracking computation ID, zone, current
  holder, checkpoint binding, execution lease, fencing token, and
  capability context
- Implement full lifecycle: suspend() quiesces to checkpoint, transfer()
  validates lease handoff and transitions to Transferring, resume()
  validates target holder and advances to Running
- All state transitions enforce fencing token monotonicity and lease
  validity

lease.rs:
- Add LeaseId type alias and LeaseHandoff struct for auditable transfer of
  exclusive execution authority between nodes
- Add LeaseTransferValidationError with 10 specific validation failure
  variants (expired, reused ID, self-transfer, holder/subject/zone/purpose
  mismatch, fencing token ordering)
- Add LeaseValidationError for basic lease integrity checks
- Implement validate_lease() and validate_lease_handoff() with full
  cross-referencing between source lease, handoff record, and current time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
